# Second Bridge Out Old Route 12

The river took the first bridge
in the spring flood of 2011,
swept the wooden pilings downstream
like matchsticks in the current.

We walked the bank that summer,
picking through debris—
a rusted mailbox, someone's
yellow raincoat caught in branches.

The second bridge stood for years
until the ice came heavy,
piled against the concrete,
pressed until something gave.

Now we cross by the third bridge,
newer, higher, built to last
they say, but we know better—
the river always wins.
